fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file to upload.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description:
The intended purpose of the uploaded file.

See the [documentation on File
purposes](/docs/api-reference/files/create#files-create-purpose).

fields.name: bytes
fields.description: The number of bytes in the file you are uploading.

fields.name: mime_type
fields.description:
The MIME type of the file.


This must fall within the supported MIME types for your file purpose. See
the supported MIME types for assistants and vision.

types.name: CreateUploadRequestPurpose
types.description:
The intended purpose of the uploaded file.

See the [documentation on File
purposes](/docs/api-reference/files/create#files-create-purpose).

types.name: FileExpirationAfter

fields.name: anchor
fields.description: Anchor timestamp after which the expiration policy applies. Supported anchors: `created_at`.

fields.name: seconds
fields.description: The number of seconds after the anchor time that the file will expire. Must be between 3600 (1 hour) and 2592000 (30 days).

types.description: The expiration policy for a file. By default, files with `purpose=batch` expire after 30 days and all other files are persisted until they are manually deleted.

types.name: Upload

fields.name: id
fields.description: The Upload unique identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the Upload was created.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file to be uploaded.

fields.name: bytes
fields.description: The intended number of bytes to be uploaded.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description: The intended purpose of the file. [Please refer here](/docs/api-reference/files/object#files/object-purpose) for acceptable values.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The status of the Upload.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the Upload will expire.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always "upload".

types.description: The Upload object can accept byte chunks in the form of Parts.

types.name: OpenAiFile

fields.name: id
fields.description: The file identifier, which can be referenced in the API endpoints.

fields.name: bytes
fields.description: The size of the file, in bytes.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the file was created.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the file will expire.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always `file`.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description: The intended purpose of the file. Supported values are `assistants`, `assistants_output`, `batch`, `batch_output`, `fine-tune`, `fine-tune-results`, `vision`, and `user_data`.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Deprecated. The current status of the file, which can be either `uploaded`, `processed`, or `error`.

fields.name: status_details
fields.description: Deprecated. For details on why a fine-tuning training file failed validation, see the `error` field on `fine_tuning.job`.

types.description: The `File` object represents a document that has been uploaded to OpenAI.

types.name: OpenAiFilePurpose
types.description: The intended purpose of the file. Supported values are `assistants`, `assistants_output`, `batch`, `batch_output`, `fine-tune`, `fine-tune-results`, `vision`, and `user_data`.

types.name: OpenAiFileStatus
types.description: Deprecated. The current status of the file, which can be either `uploaded`, `processed`, or `error`.

methods.description:
Creates an intermediate [Upload](/docs/api-reference/uploads/object) object
that you can add [Parts](/docs/api-reference/uploads/part-object) to.
Currently, an Upload can accept at most 8 GB in total and expires after an
hour after you create it.

Once you complete the Upload, we will create a
[File](/docs/api-reference/files/object) object that contains all the parts
you uploaded. This File is usable in the rest of our platform as a regular
File object.

For certain `purpose` values, the correct `mime_type` must be specified. 
Please refer to documentation for the 
[supported MIME types for your use case](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search#supported-files).

For guidance on the proper filename extensions for each purpose, please
follow the documentation on [creating a
File](/docs/api-reference/files/create).

Returns the Upload object with status `pending`.
